description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a polished, medium-brown wood grain finish. The front panel is divided into two main sections: on the left, there is a glass-covered frequency display with printed station markings, and below it are three round black control knobs. To the right of the display is a woven speaker grille, framed in a darker wood accent. The back panel is made of a dark, vented material and is labeled with 'TELEFUNKEN-SUPER FENOMEN TYP Mz 744' along with various ports and technical markings. The sides and top are smooth, continuous wood with rounded edges, giving the object a classic, streamlined appearance. This radio receiver is designed to rest on flat surfaces and would typically be found in homes, shops, or public spaces in an urban environment.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a glass frequency display, three control knobs, and a woven speaker grille. The cabinet is polished wood with rounded edges and a labeled back panel.
description_view_0: The front view shows the frequency display, three black knobs, and the woven speaker grille, all framed by polished wood.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the smooth, continuous wood surface with rounded edges and no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the dark, vented back panel with technical markings, ports, and the 'TELEFUNKEN-SUPER FENOMEN TYP Mz 744' label.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the uninterrupted wood grain and rounded corners.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
